old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-xqgc","title":"LoopbackRadio::receive ignores timeout_ms parameter","description":"File: rust/lichen-hal/src/loopback.rs:91-107

The receive() function ignores the timeout_ms parameter entirely (marked with underscore as _timeout_ms). When no packet is available, it returns None immediately instead of waiting for the specified timeout.

This violates the Radio trait contract and creates inconsistent behavior - MockRadio in lichen-embassy/mock.rs correctly sleeps for timeout_ms, but LoopbackRadio does not.

Impact: Tests using LoopbackRadio behave differently than production code. A test passing with LoopbackRadio might fail with real hardware if it depends on timing/timeout behavior.

Fix: Add async sleep/wait for timeout_ms before returning None, or at minimum poll with timeout.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:32:08.310068Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:32:08.310113Z"}

new_value: Implemented timeout in LoopbackRadio::receive using tokio sleep to match MockRadio and Radio trait; updated tests with independent timing oracle; ruff clippy cargo test passed after 3 review passes (P0-P2 only)
